# Principles of Lasting Change

**Principle 1: Trust Yourself. External experts cannot give you the answers.**

**Principle 2: Done over Perfect. Perfection is the enemy of progress.**

**Principle 3: Embrace Discomfort. Growth happens outside the comfort zone.**

**Case: The Cold Shower. A small act of deliberate discomfort builds resilience.**

**Case: The Difficult Conversation. Avoiding it makes it worse. Embracing it brings relief.**

**Case: The No. Saying no to others is saying yes to yourself.**
---

**Case: The Self-Diagnosis. A patient researches her symptoms online. She learns to trust her intuition.**
**Case: The Imperfect Start. A writer starts with bad first drafts. Progress beats perfection.**
**Case: The No. A man learns to decline invitations. He reclaims his time.**
**Case: The Setback. A patient plans for obstacles. When they come, she is ready.**
**Case: The Discomfort Challenge. A patient commits to one discomfort per day. Her resilience grows.**
**Case: The Letting Go. A woman releases the need to be right. Her relationships improve.**
**Case: The Self-Care. A woman learns that saying no to others is a form of self-care.**
**Case: The Boundaries. Setting boundaries is an act of self-respect, not selfishness.**
**Case: The Small Step. A patient makes one small change. It leads to more changes.**
